action.content: Write a funny song-style poem for kids about why you shouldn't eat a lot of sweets. The poem should have four sections, with each section marked with SECTION X.
action.label: context

action.content:
SECTION 1
Oh, the candy store is a magical place,
With gummy bears and chocolate snakes!
You might think, "I'll eat a whole big pile,
And make my tummy feel so real!"
But wait a minute, listen to the sound,
Of sugar rushing through the ground!
It's like a rocket zooming fast,
Then suddenly, it stops and crashes!

SECTION 2
Your teeth start to rattle and shake,
Like tiny drums in a crazy wake!
The sugar makes them turn to gray,
And gives you a mouth full of decay!
You'll have to brush and floss and spit,
While all your teeth are in a bit!
No more crunchy chips or crunchy toast,
Just sticky messes on your ghost!

SECTION 3
Your energy will go *zap-zap-zap*,
Like a battery that's running out of map!
You'll feel so sleepy, so heavy,
And want to nap right now, hey?
Your brain will feel like a foggy day,
With no fun games to play!
You'll trip over your own big feet,
And fall right into a sticky seat!

SECTION 4
So put the lollipops on the shelf,
And leave the chocolate bars to elf!
Eat fruits and veggies instead,
With happy smiles and healthy bread!
Your tummy will feel light and bright,
Like sunshine on a sunny night!
So save your sweets for special treats,
And keep your body strong and sweet!
action.label: response
